era_context: Continuation of the Middle Bronze Age in the Levant and Egypt; the 12th Dynasty of Egypt under Amenemhat III. In Mesopotamia, the Old Babylonian period continues under Hammurabi's successors. The Hittite Old Kingdom is consolidating power in Anatolia. The Indus Valley Civilization remains in its mature phase. In China, the Shang dynasty is in its early phase (traditional dating).

events.title: Continued reign of Amenemhat III
events.description: Amenemhat III continues his reign, overseeing the height of the 12th Dynasty's power and prosperity. This period is marked by extensive building programs, administrative efficiency, and military campaigns into Nubia and the Levant. The pharaoh's authority is reflected in contemporary inscriptions and administrative records.

sources.name: Inscriptions from temples and administrative sites
Papyri from Middle Kingdom administrative archives

events.certainty_note: Amenemhat III's reign is well-attested, but absolute dating carries ±10–15 year uncertainty as noted above.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are known for 1872 BCE that would contradict broader historical narratives. The year falls within a well-documented reign but no particular event is singled out in the historical record.
historiographic_note: 1872 BCE is not marked by any known specific event in the surviving historical record. The year is assigned to the reign of Amenemhat III by chronological convention, but no contemporary sources single out this particular year for special significance. This reflects the general sparsity of year-by-year historical documentation for the Bronze Age; most records are organized by reign or dynasty rather than by individual years.

graph_edges.note: Part of the same reign as the previous year
